perturbation.original_text: TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES ALL RIGHT OF TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION, PROCEEDING, CLAIM, OR COUNTERCLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY MATTER ARISING HEREUNDER.
perturbation.changed_text: EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ALL RIGHT OF TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION, PROCEEDING, CLAIM, OR COUNTERCLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY MATTER ARISING HEREUNDER.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW' suggests an absolute waiver of jury trial rights, regardless of any legal limitations. This could be problematic if a court finds that certain claims or counterclaims are not waivable under applicable Delaware law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article I, Section 4 of the Delaware Constitution guarantees the right to a jury trial. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. By removing the phrase 'to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law,' the contract suggests that the jury trial right is waived completely, which may not be enforceable if Delaware law prohibits the waiver in certain situations or requires a more explicit acknowledgment of the waiver.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW' creates an Omission Legal Contradiction because it omits legally mandated consumer protection. The changed text suggests an absolute waiver of the right to a jury trial, regardless of any legal limitations, which contradicts Delaware law regarding knowing and voluntary waivers as described in the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee agrees to comply with Licensor's trademark usage guidelines and any other policies and requirements applicable to the Licensed Mark.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee agrees to comply with Licensor's trademark usage guidelines.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'any other policies and requirements applicable to the Licensed Mark', the contract opens the door to Licensor imposing policies that could violate consumer protection laws. For example, Licensor could implement a policy requiring the Licensee to misrepresent the quality or origin of the Licensed Products.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware Consumer Fraud Act, 6 Del. C. В§ 2511
perturbation.law_explanation: The Delaware Consumer Fraud Act prohibits deceptive trade practices. If the omitted portion of the clause allows Licensor to enforce policies requiring Licensee to engage in deceptive practices, the contract could be seen as contributing to a violation of this Act. For instance, policies enforcing misleading claims about Licensed Product qualities would violate the Act.

Deceptive foreclosure practices. (a) No person shall make, use, or cause to be made or used a deceptive or fraudulent record, document, or statement in support of any foreclosure upon real property, including, without limitation, statements about the offering of a loan modification, the borrowerГўВЂВ™s history of payments, the validity of the assignment of the mortgage loan, the identity of the record holder of the mortgage loan, or the compliance with any other requirements of the Delaware Code or Superior Court rule. (b) The Attorney General shall have the same authority to enforce and carry out this section as is granted by Chapter 25 of Title 29 and by Г‚В§Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚2511-2527 and 2531-2536 of this title. (d) If a court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction finds that any person has wilfully violated this section, the Attorney General, upon petition to the court or tribunal, shall recover from the person, on behalf of the State, in addition to all costs, a civil penalty of not more than $10,000 per violation pursuant to Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚2533 of this title. If the violation is against an elderly person or person with a disability, an additional civil penalty of not more than $10,000 per violation shall be recovered pursuant to Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚2581 of this title. Each day that a wilful violation continues shall be considered a separate violation. (f) After confirmation of the foreclosure sale by Superior Court, title to real property sold to an innocent third-party purchaser for value at a foreclosure sale shall not be contested, clouded, or deemed to be unmarketable or uninsurable for title insurance based solely upon a violation of this section. 78 Del. Laws, c. 197, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Licensee to comply with 'any other policies and requirements applicable to the Licensed Mark.' According to the Perturbation Explanation, this omission allows the Licensor to impose policies that could violate consumer protection laws, such as policies requiring the Licensee to misrepresent the quality or origin of the Licensed Products. The Law Explanation also indicates that this could violate the Delaware Consumer Fraud Act, as deceptive practices could be enforced. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 does not explicitly mention consumer fraud, it does detail deceptive foreclosure practices and the penalties associated with wilful violations, thus illustrating the state's intent to penalize deceptive practices in general. The removal of the clause opens the door to potential deceptive practices, hence there is a legal contradiction.
